channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Fiancée of Faster Pussycat singer dies after going overboard on '80s-themed cruise, family says
item.description: Kimberly Burch, the fiancée of an '80s rock star, has died after going overboard an '80s-themed cruise, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KTLA 5

item.title: Schools are closed and public transport has stopped as rare cyclone approaches Australian coast
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — Early wind and rain from a rare tropical cyclone began lashing part of eastern Australia on Thursday as schools were closed, public transport was stopped and desperate residents got around shortages of sandbags by buying potting mix.
item.dc:creator: JOHN PYE and ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A youth theater production rises from the ashes of the Los Angeles fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The day after her house burned down, Lara Ganz sent a group message to the youth theater troupe she runs: They would not let the Los Angeles firestorm stop their upcoming show.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Women's rights are under attack 30 years after leaders adopted a blueprint for equality, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — Thirty years after world leaders adopted a historic blueprint to achieve gender equality, a new United Nations report says women’s and girls’ rights are under attack and gender discrimination remains deeply embedded in economies and societies.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Scientists raise concerns as the US stops sharing air quality data from embassies worldwide
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The U.S. government will stop sharing air quality data gathered from its embassies and consulates, worrying local scientists and experts who say the effort was vital to monitor global air quality and improve public health.
item.dc:creator: TAIWO ADEBAYO and SIBI ARASU, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance suggests other industries won't get tariff carve-outs
item.description: Vice President Vance on Wednesday said that industries other than U.S. automakers have asked for exemptions on tariffs, but suggested that the Trump administration would not extend further carve outs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: Three Michigan brothers missing since 2010 are declared dead by a judge at mom's request
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved despite an explicit belief by investigators that their father is responsible.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mysterious affliction causing endangered Florida fish to spin and die is back
item.description: Following a roughly nine-month lull, there have been 22 reports of the odd spinning behavior involving sawfish in waters around the Florida Keys since December.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health
item.description: Mötley Crüe announced they are postponing their upcoming Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KRON4

item.title: Popular cartoon voice actor and comedian dead at 67
item.description: George Lowe, a voice actor and comedian best known for portraying Space Ghost on the partially animated TV series "Space Ghost Coast to Coast," has died. Lowe, 67, passed away on Sunday, a representative for the actor confirmed to outlets including Deadline and A.V. Club.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, KTLA 5

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lutnick: Trump may announce tariff compromise with Canada, Mexico on Wednesday
item.description: Trump implementing 25 percent tariffs triggered retaliatory measures from both nations, with major corporations also warning that it could lead to potential price increases for consumers.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy John's is giving out free sandwiches. Here's how to redeem the limited-time deal
item.description: Sandwich chain Jimmy John’s is giving away $1 million worth of toasted subs on Tuesday to celebrate a new product launch.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Couple arrested after allegedly throwing coffee at airline employees, forcing way onto Cancun flight
item.description: A Florida couple were arrested after allegedly assaulting employees at an American Airlines gate at Miami International Airport Sunday when they were denied boarding for arriving late.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 46 percent say Trump changing economy for the worse: Survey
item.description: Recent polling has indicated that U.S. adults’ confidence in Trump’s ability to manage the country’s economy — the top issue in the 2024 presidential election — has been slipping.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: FAA investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Monday it is investigating multiple false collision warnings on flights approaching Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis no longer requires mechanical ventilation after respiratory crisis
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis had stabilized enough Tuesday after two respiratory crises to be taken off noninvasive mechanical ventilation and receive just high flows of supplemental oxygen, as he continued his more than two-week long fight against double pneumonia, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WGN 9

item.title: Linda McMahon confirmed as Trump's Education secretary
item.description: Linda McMahon will be the next secretary of the Department of Education after passing a full Senate vote for her confirmation on Monday. The Senate voted 51-45 long party lines to confirm President Trump’s nominee to lead a federal agency he has been vocal about wanting to shut down.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, Fox 5

item.title: US stocks tumble after Trump announces Canadian and Mexican tariffs
item.description: U.S. stocks are falling sharply Monday after President Donald Trump said tariffs he announced on Canada and Mexico will take effect on Tuesday. The bond market also took a hit following the latest warning signal on the strength of the U.S. economy.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump: 25 percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico will go into effect Tuesday
item.description: President Trump said Monday that the 25 percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico will officially go into effect Tuesday, announcing there is no room for dealmaking to delay them again. “The tariffs, they're all set.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Youngkin grants clemency to a fired Virginia police sergeant who shot and killed an unarmed man
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Days after a judge sentenced a Virginia police officer to prison in the fatal shooting of an unarmed man accused of stealing sunglasses, the state's Republican governor granted the fired officer clemency, meaning he won't have to serve further time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Black graves are being moved to make way for an industrial park, drawing a mix of emotions
item.description: DANVILLE, Va. (AP) — A decision to move the remains of hundreds African American tenant farmers from a former Virginia tobacco plantation to a dedicated burial ground has elicited a range of emotions among the sharecroppers’ descendants.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cat food recalled over risk of bird flu weeks after pets euthanized
item.description: The Wild Coast LLC product 16oz and 24oz frozen Boneless Free Range Chicken Formula raw pet food for cats was distributed to pet food retailers in Washington State and Oregon. The product is frozen in a small round white plastic container with a green label.
item.dc:creator: Erin Yudt, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bondi says she was misled on Epstein documents
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi says she was misled into believing she had all the files related to the Epstein investigations, and is now seeking additional information from the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Magnitude 4.5 earthquake strikes in Washington state
item.description: A “notable” earthquake registering at a preliminary magnitude of 4.5 struck Washington State on Monday morning, according to the United States Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Kroger CEO resigns amid personal conduct investigation
item.description: McMullen's departure comes months after Kroger's failed two-year effort to merge with another supermarket giant, Albertsons.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, Fox 5

item.title: Singer Angie Stone killed in car crash after leaving Alabama performance
item.description: Stone was riding in the back of a vehicle that "flipped over and subsequently hit by a big rig," according to family and friends.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, ABC 4

item.title: Crews battle wildfires in North and South Carolina amid dry conditions and gusty winds
item.description: Crews battled wildfires in North and South Carolina on Sunday amid dry conditions and gusty winds as residents were forced to evacuate in some areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to watch when Trump gives his big speech to Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump loves a good spectacle, and it's hard to top a speech to a joint session of Congress. The House chamber is packed with lawmakers, and the president's arrival is announced in a booming voice by the sergeant-at-arms, triggering cacophonous applause.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Strip club Cinderella story 'Anora' wins best picture at the Oscars
item.description: "Anora,” a strip club Cinderella story without the fairy tale ending, was crowned best picture at the 97th Academy Awards on Sunday, handing Sean Baker’s gritty, Brooklyn-set screwball farce Hollywood’s top prize.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada man pleads guilty to extorting Instagram users, influencers
item.description: A Las Vegas man has pleaded guilty to extorting and threatening Instagram users and influencers, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, WGN 9
